title: Die mittelenglische Sprichwörtersammlung in Douce 52
summary: CME00139 is the repository copy of a University of Michigan electronic transcription of selected portions of Max Förster's 1906 article Die mittelenglische Sprichwörtersammlung in Douce 52. The article's main section edits 150 numbered Middle English proverbs and commonplaces from Bodleian Library MS. Douce 52, fols. 13–31, while retaining Latin counterparts in only 37 numbered units. Its addenda excerpt ten English sayings or fable passages from Lucy Toulmin Smith and Paul Meyer's 1889 edition of Nicole Bozon's Contes moralisés, whose base and comparison witnesses are Gray's Inn MS 12, British Library Add MS 46919, and Harley MS 1288. The XML encodes the article title and byline plus printed pp. 44–60, but omits Förster's introduction on pp. 40–44 and relocates the title and byline from p. 40 to the p. 44 position. Complete scans and OCR of both printed source volumes, the official Bodleian TEI description, and DIMEV v1.0 are cached with fixity. No complete public facsimile was found for any of the four manuscripts.
review_notes: The cataloged subject is Förster's two-stream edited selection, not a single stable abstract medieval work and not the complete contents of MS. Douce 52.
The 150 main numbered units include 37 units in which Förster retained Latin; the ten addenda are a second independently numbered section sourced through Smith and Meyer's edition.

Förster's mid-fifteenth-century scribal date, London-language description, and hypothetical older north-west Midland or southern northern exemplar are editorial hypotheses. They are not silently converted into a work composition date or place.

The current Bodleian record gives the target as fols. 13–31 with an inferred endpoint, whereas Förster says fols. 13a–31a. A complete target-leaf facsimile is needed to resolve the exact side and physical endpoint.

Smith and Meyer's legacy Harley locus, fols. 112–125, conflicts with the current British Library locus, fols. 91r–105v. The two locators are preserved without inferring an unverified foliation offset.

The University of Michigan item header currently misidentifies CME00139 as Frühmittelenglische Sprichwörter in Englische Studien 31 (1900), while the displayed content and repository XML are Förster's 1906 Douce article. This is a discovery-layer mismatch, not a different source lineage.
DIMEV v1.0 links MS. Douce 52 to 42 distinct verse records in 43 witness occurrences; these item-level records do not make one DIMEV identifier for Förster's whole 150-unit selection.
Whole-object facsimile completeness is evaluated against each current physical manuscript, not against the few target leaves or passages selected by an edition.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00139
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's publication workflow and supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID all identify CME00139; the file is 64,244 bytes.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item CME00139
entities.description: A public SGML-derived transcription of Förster's selected article portions, preserving 150 main AB units, ten addenda AB units, 139 notes, 17 printed page breaks, 250 display-line milestones, and the encoded Middle English, Latin, German, and short Old French layers.
notes: The current U-M header's title and citation mismatch the item content; lineage is established from the encoded title, byline, pagination, notes, and the verified source scan.

entities.label: Max Förster, Die mittelenglische Sprichwörtersammlung in Douce 52 (1906)
entities.description: Förster's article, printed on pp. 40–60 of the 1906 Munich Neuphilologentag Festschrift. Its introduction occupies pp. 40–44, the 150-unit Douce selection pp. 44–57, and ten addenda pp. 58–60.

bibliographic.citation: Max Förster, 'Die mittelenglische Sprichwörtersammlung in Douce 52,' in Festschrift zum XII. Allgemeinen Deutschen Neuphilologentage in München, Pfingsten 1906, ed. Eugen Stollreither (Erlangen: Junge und Sohn, 1906), 40–60.
bibliographic.title: Die mittelenglische Sprichwörtersammlung in Douce 52
bibliographic.container_title: Festschrift zum XII. Allgemeinen Deutschen Neuphilologentage in München, Pfingsten 1906

entities.label: Festschrift zum XII. Allgemeinen Deutschen Neuphilologentage (1906)
entities.description: The complete edited Festschrift volume containing Förster's article on printed pp. 40–60.

bibliographic.citation: Festschrift zum XII. Allgemeinen Deutschen Neuphilologentage in München, Pfingsten 1906, ed. Eugen Stollreither (Erlangen: Junge und Sohn, 1906).
bibliographic.title: Festschrift zum XII. Allgemeinen Deutschen Neuphilologentage in München, Pfingsten 1906

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of the 1906 Festschrift
entities.description: A complete 550-page volume scan. Förster's article appears on PDF pages 56–76 / Internet Archive leaves n56–n76; the encoded text starts at printed p. 44 / PDF p. 60 and continues through printed p. 60 / PDF p. 76.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete-volume PDF, OCR, and scan metadata

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. Douce 52
entities.description: A fifteenth-century English parchment manuscript of i + 103 leaves. Its second cataloged item, fols. 13–31 with an inferred endpoint, contains English proverbs with Latin hexameter counterparts, some Latin-only proverbs, and a rough alphabetical order; the beginning is imperfect.

date_statements.label: Manuscript production
date_statements.notes: This dates the witness, not the composition of each saying.

notes: The binding is described as leather, perhaps over wooden boards, later recovered in green leather.
At least three hands occur; catalog Hand A writes articles 1–3 and therefore includes the target item.
Penwork decoration extends across fols. 1r–25v and therefore overlaps part of the target.
Francis Douce owned the manuscript and bequeathed it to the Bodleian in 1834.

entities.label: Medieval Manuscripts in Oxford Libraries record for MS. Douce 52
entities.description: The official detailed TEI manuscript description used for current shelfmark, target content and locus, date, languages, material, hands, decoration, binding, and provenance.

entities.label: Smith and Meyer, Les contes moralisés de Nicole Bozon (1889)
entities.description: The first edition of Nicole Bozon's Contes moralisés, based principally on Gray's Inn MS 12, collated against the former Phillipps 8336 (now Add MS 46919), and followed by the partial Latin translation in Harley MS 1288. Förster selected ten English passages from printed pp. 12, 20, 23, 54, 117, 145, 151, 205, and 209.

bibliographic.citation: Nicole Bozon, Les contes moralisés de Nicole Bozon, frère mineur, publiés pour la première fois d'après les manuscrits de Londres et de Cheltenham, ed. Lucy Toulmin Smith and Paul Meyer (Paris: Firmin Didot, 1889).
bibliographic.title: Les contes moralisés de Nicole Bozon, frère mineur

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Smith and Meyer (1889)
entities.description: A complete 424-page scan and OCR of the edition. Förster's ten addenda sources occur on PDF pages / Internet Archive leaves n96, n104, n107, n107, n138, n201, n229, n235, n289, and n293.

entities.label: Gray's Inn Library MS 12
entities.description: A fourteenth-century manuscript of 286 leaves written by several Gothic hands. Smith and Meyer used its comparatively complete Contes moralisés on fols. 15r–49v as their base text.

date_statements.label: Manuscript production
date_statements.value: Fourteenth century; Contes witness described as mid-fourteenth century

notes: Written by several Gothic hands.
The current binding is seventeenth-century calf and retains a brass chain staple.

entities.label: Gray's Inn selected images of MS 12
entities.description: Five unfoliated sample images embedded in the official manuscript page. They are a selected display, not a complete facsimile, and the target Contes folios cannot be mapped from the public labels.

entities.label: British Library Add MS 46919
entities.description: The William Herebert compilation formerly Phillipps 8336. The current catalog places the Contes moralisés on fols. 120–153b; JONAS gives fols. 120r–153v. Smith and Meyer collated this witness against Gray's Inn MS 12.

date_statements.label: Manuscript production
date_statements.notes: Smith and Meyer called the witness mid-fourteenth-century or possibly earlier; the current BL description's question mark is retained.

notes: Written by multiple hands and compiled or annotated by William Herebert.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 1288
entities.description: A fifteenth-century English manuscript containing a partial Latin translation of the Contes moralisés on current fols. 91r–105v. Smith and Meyer printed that translation on pp. 195–227 and used the legacy locus fols. 112–125.

date_statements.label: Manuscript production
date_statements.notes: Smith and Meyer called it late fourteenth-century; the current catalog date is retained alongside that historical description.

physical_description.extent: 105 leaves plus modern flyleaves and stubs
notes: Written in Gothic cursive.

entities.label: DIMEV v1.0 records attested in MS. Douce 52
entities.description: A set of 42 distinct Digital Index of Middle English Verse records with 43 Douce 52 witness occurrences. Record 759 occurs twice. These records identify verse items and parallels inside the manuscript, not Förster's complete 150-unit editorial selection.

access.access_method: Search Digital Bodleian by exact shelfmark, Summary Catalogue number, and ARKs; use Imaging Services or registered onsite consultation for unavailable leaves.
notes: Dated negative scope: the official manuscript record and Digital Bodleian API searches for exact shelfmark, SC 21626, both ARKs, and unquoted variants were audited on 2026-07-13.
The exact quoted search returned zero results; broader searches returned false positives and no MS. Douce 52 object.

access.access_method: Request a complete color reproduction of the current physical manuscript, including binding, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, every leaf, edges, and detached matter where offered; map the work separately to fols. 13–31.
access.format: Repository-supplied digital images or reference PDF, subject to quotation

access.access_method: Register as a Bodleian reader and order MS. Douce 52 for consultation under current Special Collections procedures.

access.access_method: Apply as an accredited researcher with photo identification and a reference; request permission separately for photographs or reproduction.

notes: The official record's digitised-content field was blank on 2026-07-13. The Biblissima result is metadata-only and explicitly reports no digitized object.

access.access_method: Use the Imaging Services order form with shelfmark Add MS 46919 and request a complete color photographic reproduction of the current physical manuscript, including covers, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, every leaf, edges, and detached matter where offered; identify the Contes separately at current fols. 120r–153v. Ask the Library to enumerate any surfaces it cannot supply.
access.format: Electronically delivered reference or reproduction images; original manuscripts require photography rather than direct scanning
notes: The online service is in beta, difficult or large orders may require individual discussion, and availability depends on condition, handling, retrieval, and security constraints.
Imaging Services terms and the order confirmation govern delivery and reuse; written permission may be required for copying or reproducing supplied images.

rights.basis: The pinned CME repository root LICENSE uses Unlicense wording but explicitly refers to software; its scope over the encoded medieval text, editorial transcription, and metadata is not established clearly enough to broaden.
The Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse collection applies the RightsStatements.org No Copyright–United States statement while retaining provider terms for access and reuse.
The article was published in 1906 and is in the public domain in the United States.
Internet Archive metadata supplies the Public Domain Mark 1.0 for the item; the underlying 1906 publication is public domain in the United States.
The edition was published in 1889 and is in the public domain in the United States.
Internet Archive metadata records possible-copyright-status NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT and copyright-region US, but it does not supply a content license URL for the scan files.

rights.component: Medieval linguistic content represented by the witness
rights.basis: The medieval text is public domain; rights in modern photographs, catalog text, and supplied reproductions are distinct layers.

rights.component: Modern catalog description and TEI encoding
rights.basis: The Bodleian medieval-mss source release identifies the catalogue data under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 3.0.
Bodleian distinguishes private or noncommercial academic use from publication and requires users to establish permissions for reproducing supplied images.
Gray's Inn permits short-extract photography for private study only with permission and requires separate permission for reproduction.

rights.component: Future British Library manuscript photographs or reproductions
rights.basis: British Library Imaging Services states that supplied material may be subject to intellectual-property rights and that supplied images must not be copied or reproduced without written permission, which may carry additional fees. Exact order-confirmation terms must be retained with any delivery.

rights.component: Future British Library manuscript photographs or reproductions
rights.basis: British Library Imaging Services states that supplied material may be subject to intellectual-property rights and that supplied images must not be copied or reproduced without written permission, which may carry additional fees. Exact order-confirmation terms must be retained with any delivery.
The versioned Zenodo release identifies Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International.

editorial_practices.description: Förster prints 150 numbered Middle English Douce units but retains Latin counterparts in only 37 of them: the first ten and later examples useful for meaning.
editorial_practices.source_feature: A substantially bilingual English–Latin proverb sequence, with some one-language items
editorial_practices.representation: Middle English-centered edited selection with a reduced Latin layer

editorial_practices.description: Förster uses italic type to distinguish expanded abbreviations and supplied letters rather than ordinary rhetorical emphasis.
editorial_practices.representation: Italic print segments retained as encoded typographic emphasis

editorial_practices.description: Förster states that he normalizes capitalization in the Douce transcription.
Förster states that he normalizes punctuation in the Douce transcription.
Hermann Varnhagen checked Förster's proof sheets against MS. Douce 52 and supplied Latin readings; Henry Bradley supplied credited emendations and comments.
editorial_practices.scope: Main Douce text and notes, printed pp. 44–57
editorial_practices.representation: Corrected proofs, supplied Latin readings, and credited notes

editorial_practices.description: Förster excerpts ten English passages from Smith and Meyer's printed Contes edition and apparatus rather than consulting its three manuscript witnesses directly.
editorial_practices.representation: Ten independently numbered English excerpts, with selected variants and y-to-thorn or z-to-yogh character normalization

editorial_practices.description: The U-M encoding omits Förster's introduction on printed pp. 40–44 but carries forward the p. 40 title and byline at the opening p. 44 milestone, followed by the complete encoded transcription and addenda through p. 60.
editorial_practices.scope: Encoded portions of Förster's printed pp. 40 and 44–60
editorial_practices.representation: Two DIV1 sections beginning with PB N=44 and ending on PB N=60

open_questions.question: Can the Bodleian, Gray's Inn, and British Library supply or authorize complete color facsimiles of MS. Douce 52, Gray's Inn MS 12, Add MS 46919, and Harley MS 1288, and what exact storage, Git-publication, viewer, print-on-demand, and commercial permissions would govern each delivery?
next_steps: Request each complete current physical object, not only target leaves or short extracts, including bindings, spines, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, every leaf, edges, and detached matter where offered.
Map target passages separately: Douce fols. 13–31, Gray's Inn fols. 15r–49v, Add fols. 120r–153v, and Harley current fols. 91r–105v.
Retain repository inventories, quotations, delivery correspondence, and exact license or permission terms with any supplied files.

open_questions.question: What is the exact ending side and physical boundary of the Douce proverb sequence described by the catalog as fols. 13–31 with an inferred endpoint and by Förster as fols. 13a–31a?
next_steps: Inspect complete color images of fols. 30–32 and adjacent quire evidence.
Confirm whether Förster's 31a maps to modern 31r and whether the catalog's inferred endpoint includes any part of 31v.
Keep the competing locators explicit until image or repository confirmation resolves them.

open_questions.question: How do Smith and Meyer's legacy Harley locus fols. 112–125 and date of the late fourteenth century correspond to the current British Library locus fols. 91r–105v and fifteenth-century date, and should any Add MS 46919 locator or date differences also be normalized?
next_steps: Consult current manuscript foliation images or obtain repository confirmation before inferring an offset.
Preserve Smith and Meyer's historical locators and dates as edition-layer claims.
Use current repository locators for access requests while recording all delivery labels verbatim.

open_questions.question: Will the University of Michigan correct CME00139's current item-header title and citation so that discovery metadata identifies Förster's 1906 Douce article?
next_steps: Report the mismatch with the stable item identifier, encoded title/byline, and verified printed citation.
Do not model the incorrect header as an alternate source edition.
Recheck the live item after any provider response.

open_questions.question: Which of Förster's 150 numbered Douce units correspond exactly to the 42 distinct DIMEV v1.0 records, and how should prose commonplaces, Latin-only manuscript material, and repeated verse records be represented in a future item-level concordance?
next_steps: Build a numbered Förster-to-DIMEV concordance from incipits, explicits, loci, and the complete target folios.
Retain record-759's two witness occurrences rather than deduplicating the manuscript evidence.
Do not assign one DIMEV record to the full edited selection.
